current_best_integrated_proxy.artifact_read: metrics.md retained fixed-router packed-sim BF16-contract S4096 run
current_best_integrated_proxy.caveat: Fixed-router packed-sim is the fastest current math proxy, but it is not final learned-router training behavior.

normal_loss_backward_profiler_cross_chec.scope: one-iteration torch.profiler trace of the same S4096 fixed-router retained path using normal loss.backward
normal_loss_backward_profiler_cross_chec.read: This cross-check replaces the earlier rank-0 uncertainty with a normal backward sanity check. It still is not a perfect additive subsystem tree, but it confirms that the backward phase is dominated by GEMM/BMM-family kernels and that copy/cast movement is secondary.

exact_phase_gap_read.read: The current forward pass alone consumes about 90.7% of the whole 24.9 ms layer budget, and current backward alone is about 1.83x the whole target. Closing the gap therefore requires both attention/LI backward and MoE training-GEMM replacement; a dispatch-only or forward-only tweak cannot close 43.31 ms.

current_forward_breakdown.read: The retained forward sparse-MLA path is still 3.39 ms above the 3.6 ms forward anchor, and routed-MoE forward alone already uses about three quarters of the entire 12.9 ms MoE fwd+bwd anchor.

backward_attribution_non_additive.source: metrics.md S4096 1-iter retained-path segmented backward attribution
backward_attribution_non_additive.warning: The following block timings are segmented attribution probes and are not additive; dgrad and wgrad rows rerun slices of the backward graph to rank bottleneck families.
backward_attribution_non_additive.read: The ranking says MoE routed dgrad/wgrad are the largest current fixed-router training families, while sparse-MLA backward remains a large attention-side family. These rows should guide kernel work, not be summed as a full-layer total.

planning_budget_ms.basis: 3.6 ms forward anchor plus prior about 5.07 ms sparse-MLA backward anchor, with only small allowance for LI/boundary integration.

route_slice.read: The compressed top-k route dominates the raw query-major shared-KV backward slice; local sliding-window attention is not the first wall.

retired_inverse_route_dkv_reduction.decision: Do not pursue the key-major scratch two-pass dKV reduction as the production sparse MLA backward route. It removes atomics and tightens numerics, but loses query/key reuse and is 10x+ slower than the retained query-major raw slice at S4096.

attention_li_current_evidence.read: The current attention plus LI diagnostic floor already exceeds the entire 24.9 ms layer target before MoE, residual/RMSNorm, compressor backward integration, and layer bookkeeping.

dynamic_moe_bridge_isolated_s4096.caveat: Isolated op medians, not an end-to-end fused latency prediction.

moe_current_evidence.aiter_dynamic_gmm_read: AITER BF16 compact grouped-GEMM probes are correctness-clean but slower than the retained padded torch.bmm/hipBLASLt path for these training contractions, so the current AITER wrapper is not the missing win.
moe_current_evidence.read: The MoE issue is padded/ragged grouped GEMM math and tile efficiency, not mainly dispatch/combine glue. At S4096, route build, gather, score grad, and reduce pieces are small compared with routed GEMM families.

gap_ladder.item: Keep the fresh S4096 torch-profiler trace as a normal-backward cross-check
gap_ladder.current_signal: Profiler trace measured 71.04 ms total with 24.77 ms forward and 46.12 ms backward; phase kernel sums show backward at 36.17 ms Cijk GEMM-family kernels, 3.55 ms copy/cast, 1.96 ms elementwise, and 1.68 ms softmax.
gap_ladder.why: The exact 68.21 ms run remains the clean baseline for gap math, but the normal loss.backward trace confirms the segmented ranking without relying only on repeated autograd.grad probes.
gap_ladder.remaining_caveat: The trace does not yet provide a perfect additive backward tree split into attention backward versus MoE backward, so segmented rows still guide subsystem-specific kernel work.

gap_ladder.item: Treat SGLang DSv4 forward as a fusion reference, not a training-latency oracle
gap_ladder.current_signal: The image-local run_dsv4.sh selects DeepSeek-V4-Pro, TP=8, compressed attention, FP8 KV-cache Triton path, fused compress, TileLang indexer, AITER MHC pre/post, fused hash top-k, fused WQA/WKV, and inference MoE knobs.
gap_ladder.target_signal: Port the compatible forward-boundary fusions into the BF16 TorchTitan training contract while keeping autograd and no-selected-state backward constraints.
gap_ladder.expected_role_in_gap: This sharpens the forward side: the current 22.58 ms forward is a real training proxy number, but SGLang says it should be decomposed into attention-boundary, MoE forward, and boundary/bookkeeping fusions rather than treated as one irreducible wall.
gap_ladder.implementation_shape: Use the current 4.31-4.47 ms TorchTitan-shaped attention-boundary forward profile as the forward attention guide; focus the remaining forward gap on MoE forward tile efficiency and SGLang-style q/wkv/compress/indexer fusion.

gap_ladder.item: Replace the shared-KV sparse-MLA backward compressed top-k route
gap_ladder.current_signal: Raw query-major full local+compressed backward is 14.39 ms; compressed-only is 11.02 ms. A structured inverse-route dKV two-pass reduction is retired: its best S4096 point is 140.67 ms and the lower-scratch extended sweep is 432-483 ms.
gap_ladder.target_signal: Move raw sparse-MLA backward toward the prior about 5.07 ms sparse-MLA backward anchor.
gap_ladder.expected_role_in_gap: Primary attention-side reduction, roughly high single-digit milliseconds before full boundary integration effects.
gap_ladder.implementation_shape: Fused compressed top-k dKV/scatter with dynamic selected-group reuse; keep query-major QK/probability/PV reuse and the no selected K/V or probability saved-state contract.

gap_ladder.item: Fuse LI target-probs, KL derivative, and LI dQ/dK/dW into sparse MLA/compressor backward
gap_ladder.current_signal: Wide-tile unfused LI pipeline is 4.49 ms, with LI grads about 3.18 ms and d_index_k about 2.71 ms.
gap_ladder.target_signal: Make LI a fused side product of sparse MLA/compressor backward rather than a separate diagnostic pipeline.
gap_ladder.expected_role_in_gap: Recover a few milliseconds and remove intermediate tensors/launches that block the attention boundary from fitting in the layer budget.

gap_ladder.item: Drive the full attention plus LI boundary to an about 9-10 ms planning budget
gap_ladder.current_signal: Shared-KV attention boundary fwd+bwd is 23.36 ms; adding unfused LI gives a 27.85 ms diagnostic floor.
gap_ladder.target_signal: Forward around 3.6-4.5 ms, backward near the prior about 5 ms class, and LI integrated cheaply.
gap_ladder.expected_role_in_gap: This is the attention-side must-have; without it, attention plus LI alone is already 2.95 ms over the full-layer target.

gap_ladder.item: Build compact-native grouped MoE training GEMM/backward
gap_ladder.current_signal: Fixed-router routed-MoE forward is 9.66 ms; segmented routed dgrad/wgrad are about 26 ms each; dynamic isolated MoE train-path sum is about 46.34 ms.
gap_ladder.target_signal: Whole routed expert GEMM family fwd+bwd around the 12.9 ms anchor.
gap_ladder.expected_role_in_gap: Primary MoE-side reduction. Dispatch/combine-only wrappers cannot close the gap because route/reduce glue is small relative to GEMM families.
gap_ladder.implementation_shape: Consume group sizes/offsets directly, avoid max-padding, fuse activation gather, W2 dgrad, W1/W3 xgrad, W1/W2/W3 wgrad, and token-gradient reduce around a resident expert-weight layout.

gap_ladder.item: Clean boundary RMSNorm/residual/projection/bookkeeping after the math walls move
gap_ladder.current_signal: Current forward other is about 5.93 ms; older boundary backward rows are low single-digit milliseconds.
gap_ladder.target_signal: Leave only about 1-2 ms for residual/RMSNorm/bookkeeping in the final 24.9 ms layer budget.
gap_ladder.expected_role_in_gap: Final mop-up after attention/LI and MoE training GEMM are no longer dominant.

read: The 68.21 -> 24.9 ms gap is not attributable to one wrapper. The current evidence says the 22.58 ms forward is a valid TorchTitan training-proxy number, but SGLang's DSv4 AMD runtime is an inference/TP8/FP8 KV-cache fusion reference rather than a direct training-latency oracle. Attention/LI must still drop from a 27.85 ms diagnostic floor into roughly a 9-10 ms budget, and MoE routed training GEMMs must move from padded/ragged BMM-style contractions toward the 12.9 ms whole-MoE fwd+bwd anchor. Those two changes are the ladder; dispatch/combine cleanup and boundary fusion are secondary.
